evals.name: kmp-app-graph
evals.prompt: Wire up Metro DI for our KMP/Compose app. Need a single AppGraph with: Ktor HttpClient (singleton), SQLDelight database (singleton), AuthRepository, UserRepository, two ViewModels (LoginViewModel, FeedViewModel). Platform-specific bits: HttpClient engine (OkHttp on Android, Darwin on iOS), DB driver per platform. Show the graph definition, BindingContainers, providers, and how Android/iOS entry points resolve the graph.
evals.expected_output: @DependencyGraph AppGraph with @Provides or @Inject, expect/actual or per-platform @BindingContainer for HttpClient engine + DB driver, @SingleIn scopes where appropriate, ViewModels injected, Android Application + iOS entry point using createGraphFactory<AppGraph>.
assertions: Platform-specific HttpClient engine (OkHttp Android, Darwin iOS) via expect/actual or per-platform BindingContainer

evals.name: feature-subgraph
evals.prompt: Adding a new Settings feature to our KMP app. Want it as a separate Gradle module with its own Metro subgraph extending the AppGraph — so it can use AppGraph's HttpClient and AuthRepository, but adds its own SettingsRepository + SettingsViewModel. Show the GraphExtension definition, BindingContainer for the feature, and how the parent graph exposes what's needed.
evals.expected_output: @GraphExtension SettingsGraph with parent injection, @BindingContainer with SettingsRepository binding, parent AppGraph exposes HttpClient+AuthRepository (via @Provides on graph or @Includes), factory pattern for creating subgraph from parent.
assertions: Parent graph access (AppGraph as @Includes or via factory parameter)
Parent AppGraph @Provides exposes HttpClient + AuthRepository so subgraph can use them
Factory pattern for creating subgraph from parent
